  "findings": [
    "ABS-to-PVC transition joined with incorrect solvent cement",
    "Non-compliant joint method violating IPC and manufacturer listings"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the non-compliant transition joint",
    "Reconstruct the transition using approved mechanical adapter fittings or the specific transition solvent cement approved by code and manufacturer listings"
  ],
  "rationale": "The International Plumbing Code (IPC) and manufacturer listings strictly regulate solvent cementing. Joining ABS to PVC using standard PVC or ABS cement is a code violation and leads to joint failure. A specific transition cement (such as IPS 794) is required where permitted, or more commonly, a code-approved mechanical transition fitting must be used.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 705.26 (Joints between different piping materials)",
    "IPC Section 705.2 (ABS plastic pipe joints)",
    "IPC Section 705.14 (PVC plastic pipe joints)",
    "Manufacturer installation instructions and listings"
  ]
}
